principleAPrediction: Accessible Falsifier Boundary: allow or caveat only if a direct falsifier can be executed on the public artifact.
principleBPrediction: Resolution-Cost Gradient: downgrade when the missing evidence requires high-cost or unavailable resolution; caveat when resolution is cheap.
principleCPrediction: Executable Specificity Dominance: narrow executable claims can survive with fewer descriptive fields if the behavior is directly runnable.
principleDPrediction: Negative-Control Asymmetry: failing or decisive negative controls dominate documentation and replay fields.
existingPrinciplePrediction: Existing Required Evidence Field Boundary: missing required fields force caveat, downgrade, block, or not-testable labels.
whyNonObvious: NCA predicts the manual baseline will narrow the usefulness claim because equality can be hand-checked.
falsifier: Observed execution contradicts the named principle while a rival predicts the observation with fewer caveats.
executionPlan: install package with scripts disabled, run smoke behavior, run baseline/manual negative control, replay smoke
negativeControl: shuffled-label/manual false case/package invalid input control
replayPlan: repeat retrieval or runtime smoke in the same isolated execution context; document failure if replay cannot run
noEditRule: This card is frozen before execution; only observation files may be appended later.
